heading: Agricultural trade promotion and facilitation
label: 5623 Agricultural trade promotion and facilitation

children.text: to access, develop, maintain, and expand markets for United States agricultural commodities; and
to promote cooperation and the exchange of information.

children.label: A
children.text: a United States agricultural trade organization or regional State-related organization that promotes the export and sale of United States agricultural commodities and that does not stand to profit directly from specific sales of United States agricultural commodities;

children.label: B
children.text: a cooperative organization or State agency that promotes the sale of United States agricultural commodities; or

children.label: C
children.text: a private organization that promotes the export and sale of United States agricultural commodities if the Secretary determines that such organization would significantly contribute to United States export market development.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 2 In general
children.text: The Commodity Credit Corporation shall establish and carry out a program, to be known as the “Market Access Program”, to encourage the development, maintenance, and expansion of commercial export markets for United States agricultural commodities (including commodities that are organically produced (as defined in )) through cost-share assistance to eligible trade organizations that implement a foreign market development program.section 6502 of this title

children.heading: Marketing plan and other requirements
children.label: A Marketing plan and other requirements
i
children.text: prepare and submit a marketing plan to the Secretary that meets the guidelines governing such a marketing plan specified in this paragraph or otherwise established by the Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: meet any other requirements established by the Secretary; and

children.label: iii
children.text: enter into an agreement with the Secretary.

children.heading: Purpose of marketing plan
children.label: B Purpose of marketing plan
children.text: A marketing plan submitted under this paragraph shall describe the advertising or other market oriented export promotion activities to be carried out by the eligible trade organization with respect to which assistance under this subsection is being requested.

children.label: i
children.text: specifically describe the manner in which assistance received by the eligible trade organization, in conjunction with funds and services provided by the eligible trade organization, will be expended in implementing the marketing plan;

children.label: ii
children.text: establish specific market goals to be achieved under the marketing plan; and

children.label: iii
children.text: contain whatever additional requirements are determined by the Secretary to be necessary.

children.heading: Branded promotion
children.label: D Branded promotion
children.text: A marketing plan approved by the Secretary may provide for the use of branded advertising to promote the sale of United States agricultural commodities in a foreign country under such terms and conditions as may be established by the Secretary.

children.heading: Amendments
children.label: E Amendments
children.text: An approved marketing plan may be amended by the eligible trade organization at any time, subject to the approval of the amendment by the Secretary.

children.heading: Level of assistance and cost-share requirements
children.label: 4 Level of assistance and cost-share requirements

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall justify in writing the level of assistance to be provided to an eligible trade organization under this subsection and the level of cost sharing required of the organization.

children.heading: Limitation on branded promotion
children.label: B Limitation on branded promotion
children.text: Assistance provided under this subsection for activities described in paragraph (3)(D) shall not exceed 50 percent of the cost of implementing the marketing plan, except that the Secretary may determine not to apply such limitation in the case of United States agricultural commodities with respect to which there has been a favorable decision by the United States Trade Representative under . Criteria used by the Secretary for determining that the limitation shall not apply shall be consistent and documented.section 2411 of title 19

children.heading: Multiyear basis
children.label: A Multiyear basis
children.text: The Secretary may provide assistance under this subsection on a multiyear basis, subject to annual review by the Secretary for compliance with the approved marketing plan.

children.label: i
children.text: the eligible trade organization is not adhering to the terms and conditions applicable to the provision of the assistance;

children.label: ii
children.text: the eligible trade organization is not implementing the approved marketing plan or is not adequately meeting the established goals of the plan;

children.label: iii
children.text: the eligible trade organization is not adequately contributing its own resources to the implementation of the plan; or

children.label: iv
children.text: the Secretary determines that termination of assistance in a particular instance is in the best interests of the Market Access Program.

children.label: i
children.text: An evaluation of the effectiveness of the marketing plan of the eligible trade organization in developing or maintaining markets for United States agricultural commodities.

children.label: ii
children.text: An evaluation of whether assistance provided under this subsection is necessary to maintain such markets.

children.label: iii
children.text: A thorough accounting of the expenditure by the eligible trade organization of the assistance provided under this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: to provide direct assistance to any foreign for-profit corporation for the corporation’s use in promoting foreign-produced products; or

children.label: i
children.text: a cooperative;

children.label: ii
children.text: an association described in ; orsection 291 of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: a nonprofit trade association.

children.heading: Permissive use of funds
children.label: 7 Permissive use of funds
children.text: Assistance provided under this subsection to a United States agricultural trade association, cooperative, or small business may be used for individual branded promotional activity related to a United States branded product, if the beneficiaries of the activity have provided funds for the activity in an amount that is at least equivalent to the amount of such assistance.

children.heading: Priority
children.label: 8 Priority
children.text: In providing assistance for branded promotion, the Secretary should give priority to small-sized entities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary should require a minimum contribution level of 10 percent from an eligible trade organization that receives assistance for nonbranded promotion.

children.heading: Increases in contribution level
children.label: B Increases in contribution level
children.text: The Secretary may increase the contribution level in any subsequent year that an eligible trade organization receives assistance for nonbranded promotion.

children.heading: Additionality
children.label: 10 Additionality
children.text: The Secretary should require each participant in the Market Access Program to certify that any Federal funds received supplement, but do not supplant, private or third party participant funds or other contributions to Program activities.

children.heading: Independent audits
children.label: 11 Independent audits
children.text: If as a result of an evaluation or audit of activities of a participant under the Market Access Program, the Secretary determines that a further review is justified in order to ensure compliance with the requirements of the Program, the Secretary should require the participant to contract for an independent audit of the Program activities, including activities of any subcontractor.

children.heading: Tobacco
children.label: 12 Tobacco
children.text: No funds made available under the Market Access Program may be used for activities to develop, maintain, or expand foreign markets for tobacco.

children.heading: Foreign Market Development Cooperator Program
children.label: c Foreign Market Development Cooperator Program
A
children.text: promotes the export of 1 or more United States agricultural commodities; and

children.label: B
children.text: does not have a business interest in or receive remuneration from specific sales of agricultural commodities.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 2 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish and, in cooperation with eligible trade organizations, carry out a program to be known as the “Foreign Market Development Cooperator Program” to maintain and develop foreign markets for United States agricultural commodities.

children.label: A
children.text: cost-share assistance to an eligible trade organization under a contract or agreement with the eligible trade organization; and

children.label: B
children.text: assistance for other costs that are appropriate to carry out the Foreign Market Development Cooperator Program, including contingent liabilities that are not otherwise funded.

children.heading: E (Kika) de la Garza Emerging Markets Program
children.label: d E (Kika) de la Garza Emerging Markets Program
A
children.text: is taking steps toward a market-oriented economy through the food, agriculture, or rural business sectors of its economy; and

children.label: B
children.text: has the potential to provide a viable and significant market for United States agricultural commodities.

children.label: A
children.text: to develop agricultural markets in emerging markets; and

children.label: B
children.text: to promote cooperation and exchange of information between agricultural institutions and agribusinesses in the United States and emerging markets.

children.label: I
children.text: to make assessments of food and rural business systems needs;

children.label: II
children.text: to make recommendations on measures necessary to enhance the effectiveness of the food and rural business systems described in subclause (I), including potential reductions in trade barriers; and

children.label: III
children.text: to identify and carry out specific opportunities and projects to enhance the effectiveness of the food and rural business systems described in subclause (I).

children.heading: Extent of program
children.label: ii Extent of program
children.text: The Secretary shall implement this subparagraph with respect to at least 3 emerging markets in each fiscal year.

children.heading: Experts from the United States
children.label: B Experts from the United States
i
children.text: assistance to teams (consisting primarily of agricultural consultants, agricultural producers, other persons from the private sector, and government officials expert in assessing the food and rural business systems of other countries) to enable those teams to conduct the assessments, make the recommendations, and identify the opportunities and projects described in subparagraph (A)(i) in emerging markets;

children.label: I
children.text: United States food and rural business system experts, including United States agricultural producers and other United States individuals knowledgeable in agricultural and agribusiness matters, to enable such United States food and rural business system experts to assist in transferring knowledge and expertise to entities from emerging markets; and

children.label: II
children.text: individuals designated by emerging markets to enable such designated individuals to consult with such United States experts to enhance food and rural business systems of such emerging markets and to transfer knowledge and expertise to such emerging markets.

children.heading: Cost-sharing
children.label: C Cost-sharing
children.text: The Secretary shall encourage the nongovernmental experts described in subparagraph (B) to share the costs of, and otherwise assist in, the participation of those experts in the E (Kika) de la Garza Emerging Markets Program.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: D Technical assistance
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to provide, or pay the necessary costs for, technical assistance (including the establishment of extension services) to enable individuals or other entities to carry out recommendations, projects, and opportunities in emerging markets, including recommendations, projects, and opportunities described in subclauses (II) and (III) of subparagraph (A)(i).

children.heading: Reports to Secretary
children.label: E Reports to Secretary
children.text: A team that receives assistance under subparagraph (B)(i) shall prepare and submit to the Secretary such reports as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Advisory committee
children.label: F Advisory committee
children.text: To provide the Secretary with information that may be useful to the Secretary in carrying out this subsection, the Secretary may establish an advisory committee composed of representatives of the various sectors of the food and rural business systems of the United States.

children.heading: Effect
children.label: G Effect
children.text: The authority provided under this subsection shall be in addition to and not in place of any other authority of the Secretary or the Commodity Credit Corporation.

children.heading: Technical assistance for specialty crops
children.label: e Technical assistance for specialty crops

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture shall establish an export assistance program, in this subsection referred to as the “program”, to address existing or potential unique barriers that prohibit or threaten the export of United States specialty crops.

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 2 Purpose
children.text: The program shall provide direct assistance through public and private sector projects and technical assistance, including through the program under , to remove, resolve, or mitigate existing or potential sanitary, phytosanitary, and technical barriers to trade.section 3157(e) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: trade effect on market retention, market access, and market expansion; and

children.label: B
children.text: trade impact.

children.heading: Multiyear projects
children.label: 4 Multiyear projects
children.text: The Secretary may provide assistance under the program to a project for longer than a 5-year period if the Secretary determines that further assistance would effectively support the purpose described in paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: conduct outreach to inform eligible organizations of the requirements of the program and the process by which such organizations may submit proposals for funding;

children.label: B
children.text: provide technical assistance to eligible organizations to assist in developing proposals and complying with the requirements of the program; and

children.label: C
children.text: solicit input from eligible organizations on improvements to streamline and facilitate the provision of assistance under this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall review program regulations, procedures, and guidelines for assistance under this subsection and make revisions to streamline, improve, and clarify the application, approval and compliance processes for such assistance, including revisions to implement the requirements of paragraph (5).December 20, 2018

children.label: i
children.text: establishing accountability standards that are appropriate for the size and scope of a project; and

children.label: ii
children.text: establishing streamlined application and approval processes, including for smaller-scale projects or projects to address time-sensitive trade barriers.

children.label: i
children.text: significant sanitary or phytosanitary issue;

children.label: ii
children.text: trade barrier; or

children.label: iii
children.text: emerging sanitary or phytosanitary issue or trade barrier; and

children.label: i
children.text: any funds provided under subsection (f)(3)(A)(iv) that were not obligated in a fiscal year; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the reason such funds were not obligated.

children.heading: Commodity Credit Corporation
children.label: 1 Commodity Credit Corporation
children.text: The Secretary shall use the funds, facilities, and authorities of the Commodity Credit Corporation to carry out this section.

children.heading: Funding amount
children.label: 2 Funding amount
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023, of the funds of, or an equal value of commodities owned by, the Commodity Credit Corporation, the Secretary shall use to carry out this section $255,000,000, to remain available until expended.

children.heading: Market access program
children.label: i Market access program
children.text: For market access activities authorized under subsection (b), of the funds of, or an equal value of commodities owned by, the Commodity Credit Corporation, not less than $200,000,000 for each fiscal year.

children.heading: Foreign market development cooperator program
children.label: ii Foreign market development cooperator program
children.text: To carry out subsection (c), of the funds of, or an equal value of commodities owned by, the Commodity Credit Corporation, not less than $34,500,000 for each fiscal year.

children.heading: E (Kika) de la Garza Emerging Markets Program
children.label: iii E (Kika) de la Garza Emerging Markets Program
children.text: To provide assistance under subsection (d), of the funds of, or an equal value of commodities owned by, the Commodity Credit Corporation, not more than $8,000,000 for each fiscal year.

children.heading: Technical assistance for specialty crops
children.label: iv Technical assistance for specialty crops
children.text: To carry out subsection (e), of the funds of, or an equal value of the commodities owned by, the Commodity Credit Corporation, $9,000,000 for each fiscal year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: In addition to the amounts allocated under clauses (i) through (iv), and notwithstanding any limitations in those clauses, as determined by the Secretary, for 1 or more programs under this section for authorized activities to access, develop, maintain, and expand markets for United States agricultural commodities, $3,500,000 for each fiscal year.

children.heading: Considerations
children.label: II Considerations
children.text: In allocating funds made available under subclause (I), the Secretary may consider providing a greater allocation to 1 or more programs under this section for which the amounts requested under applications exceed available funding for the 1 or more programs.

children.heading: Reallocation
children.label: B Reallocation
children.text: Any funds allocated under clauses (i) through (iv) of subparagraph (A) that remain unobligated one year after the end of the fiscal year in which they are first made available shall be reallocated to the priority trade fund under subparagraph (A)(v). To the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall allocate such reallocated funds to support exports of those types of United States agricultural commodities eligible for assistance under the program for which the funds were originally allocated under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Cuba
children.label: 4 Cuba
children.text: Notwithstanding or any other provision of law, funds made available under this section may be used to carry out the programs authorized under subsections (b) and (c) in Cuba. Funds may not be used as described in the previous sentence in contravention with directives set forth under the National Security Presidential Memorandum entitled “Strengthening the Policy of the United States Toward Cuba” issued by the President on , during the period in which that memorandum is in effect.section 7207 of title 22June 16, 2017

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 5 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: In addition to any other amounts provided under this subsection, there are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out the programs and authorities under paragraph (3)(A)(v) and subsections (b) through (e).
